paper_title: Characteristics of δD and δ18O of Reclaimed Mine Soil Water Profile and Its Source Water Bodies in a Coal Mining Subsidence Area with High Groundwater Level—A Case Study from the Longdong Coal Mining Subsidence Area in Jiangsu Province, China

methods.description: Procedures for extracting soil moisture and analyzing liquid water samples for δD and δ18O content using laboratory equipment, including vacuum distillation for extraction and a liquid water isotope analyzer for measurement.

steps.description: Based on the theory of vacuum distillation, the LI-2000 cryogenic vacuum distillation device (Shanghai Analysys Instrument Equipment Co., Ltd, Shanghai, China) for plants and soil moisture was used to extract soil moisture, the extraction time was at least 2 h long to ensure soil moisture was entirely extracted to avoid experimental errors from incomplete extraction. In addition, the extraction process was conducted one more time in the case where the amount of extracted water was less than 1 mL.
steps.searchable_summary: Extract soil moisture from samples using vacuum distillation with a LI-2000 device.

steps.description: In this study, the LGR908-0008 Liquid Water Isotope Analyzer was used to detect the content of δD and δ 18 O. As for the precision of this instrument, 18 O/16O and D/H are above 0.1% and above 0.3% , respectively. The isotopic vales presented in this paper values were normalized against V-SMOW. The analysis was conducted following the procedure of Orlowski et al. [44].
steps.searchable_summary: Measure δD and δ18O isotopic ratios in liquid water samples using a liquid water isotope analyzer and normalize against V-SMOW.

methods.description: Statistical and conceptual analysis of δD and δ18O isotopic data, including significance testing, comparison with meteoric water lines, and calculation/interpretation of d-excess.

steps.description: The statistical analysis method used in this paper is the method suggested by Boschetti et al. [45]. We used a t-student test to verify if differences are statistically significant.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform t-student test to determine statistical significance of differences in isotopic data.

steps.description: The relationship between δD and δ 18 O is defined as meteoric water line [46]. The global meteoric water line was first proposed by Craig... Gourcy et al. [47] further modified GMWL... The above GMWL, local MWL (LMWL), and d-excess values were used in this study for water isotopic data analyzing and water sources tracing.
steps.searchable_summary: Compare measured δD and δ18O values against Global Meteoric Water Line (GMWL) and Local Meteoric Water Line (LMWL) equations.

steps.description: Dansgaard further defined the concept of d-excess in 1964 (d = δD − 8δ 18 O) [48], which can be used to evaluate the degree of deviation of δD and δ 18 O from the GMWL, and consequently be useful for determining the thermodynamic conditions, water vapor equilibrium condition, geographical condition, and climate pattern of the vapor sources... The value of d-excess was found to equal to 10 GMWL... The above GMWL, local MWL (LMWL), and d-excess values were used in this study for water isotopic data analyzing and water sources tracing.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate d-excess values from δD and δ18O measurements and interpret their significance for moisture source conditions.
